summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, regulation.

articles.title: Banks bring BNPL rivalry
articles.description: Buy now, pay later players, including Klarna and Affirm, are facing more competition as financial institutions begin offering installment payment options.

articles.title: Illinois action may curb fraud
articles.description: A new law expected to be enacted by the state would force social media companies to better protect minors online, including from financial harm.

articles.title: Fiserv’s new CEO retains team
articles.description: Aside from naming a new chief executive on Monday, the processor also took steps to retain other top executives.

articles.title: Apple delves deeper into payments
articles.description: The iPhone maker is using artificial intelligence to simplify payments, changing its consumer wallet and payment technology.

articles.title: Nuvei to buy Payoneer for $2.75B
articles.description: The Canadian payments processor will acquire the New York company to create a bigger cross-border payments player.

articles.title: Fiserv CEO exits after rocky year
articles.description: The payments giant promoted Co-President Takis Georgakopoulos to the top role Monday as Mike Lyons leaves to return to the banking industry.

articles.title: Reshaping the future of commerce, one payment at a time
articles.description: See how to turn checkout into a strategic engine for growth and loyalty with solutions from Adobe and PayPal.

articles.title: Adyen to buy Orb for $335M
articles.description: The Dutch payments processor is swallowing the San Francisco startup as both adapt their businesses to the age of artificial intelligence.
